questions.text: Who developed the Kingdom Protoctista for microscopic examination in 1861?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Earnst Haeckel, was a German biologist who is known for his work on the classification of microorganisms and coined the term "Protista" (not Protoctista) in 1866. His work was important in advancing the understanding of these microscopic organisms, but he did so in 1866, not 1861.
Herbert Faulkner Copeland was an American biologist who contributed to the field of protistology, but he is not known for developing the Kingdom Protoctista in 1861.

Lynn Margulis and K. V. Schwartz were not responsible for developing the Kingdom Protoctista in 1861. In fact, their work is more recent and not associated with the creation of this classification in the 19th century.

John Hogg was a British microscopist and naturalist who wrote extensively about microscopic life in the mid-19th century. He is known for his work in the field of protozoology and did contribute to the understanding of microorganisms, but it's not widely recognized that he developed the specific classification of the Kingdom Protoctista in 1861.

questions.explanation: A. Suggested creating the Kingdom Protista to include bacteria and other microorganisms that do not fit into the Kingdom AnimaliaB. Elevated the prokaryotes to kingdom statusC. Modifies five kingdom system

questions.text: A biologist was studying some groups of protozoa when he noticed details of some of the organisms of this kingdom, which one pair is mismatched?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This pair is mismatched. Amoebas and Euglena are different types of protozoa with distinct characteristics. Amoebas are known for their amoeboid movement, while Euglena is a flagellated protozoan.
This pair is matched. Actinopods are a group of protozoa, and Radiolarians are a specific type of actinopod.
This pair is mismatched. Foraminifera and Vorticella are two different types of protozoa. Foraminifera are marine protozoa with distinctive shells, while Vorticella is a ciliate protozoan.
This pair is matched. Ciliates are a group of protozoa known for their cilia, and Paramecium is a well-known ciliate protozoan.

questions.explanation: A. Both are ProtistsB. Both are ProtozoansC. Rhizaria-ChromalveolataD. Both are Protozoans

questions.text: Which one of these unicellular protozoa cause dysentery in human?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Entamoeba histolytica is a parasitic amoeba that can infect the human colon and cause a disease known as amoebic dysentery. It is responsible for a type of dysentery characterized by severe diarrhea with blood and mucus in the stool.
Amoeba is a general term for various types of unicellular protozoa, including Entamoeba histolytica. While some amoebas are harmless, others can be pathogenic.
Actinopods are a group of protozoa, but they are not typically associated with causing dysentery in humans.
Ciliates are another group of protozoa, and while some may cause gastrointestinal issues, they are not the primary cause of dysentery in humans.

questions.explanation: Dysentery is an inflammation of the intestine causing diarrhea with blood

questions.text: The kingdom protists consists of four main groups of eukaryotic organisms which include all these except:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Protozoans are a group of unicellular eukaryotic organisms found within the Kingdom Protista. They are typically characterized by their motility and are considered part of the Protista kingdom.
Algae, including various types of unicellular and multicellular photosynthetic organisms like green algae, red algae, and brown algae, are also part of the Kingdom Protista.
Slime molds are often classified within the Kingdom Protista, particularly the group known as plasmodial slime molds. They are known for their unique life cycles and characteristics.

Fungi are not part of the Kingdom Protista. They belong to their separate kingdom, the Kingdom of Fungi. Fungi are more closely related to animals than they are to plants, and they are characterized by their heterotrophic mode of nutrition and the presence of chitin in their cell walls.

questions.explanation: A. Unicellular nimal like protistsB. Unicellular and multicellular plant like protistsC. Fungus like protist
hints: four main groups of eukaryotic organisms

questions.text: Zooflagellates are one of the groups of protozoa. Which of the following features is NOT typically associated with zooflagellates?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Many zooflagellates have spherical or elongated body shapes. This feature is commonly associated with them.

options.text: They possess from one to many whip-like flagella
options.explanation: Zooflagellates are known for their flagella, which are whip-like appendages that they use for movement. The presence of flagella is a characteristic feature of zooflagellates.
This statement is NOT typically associated with zooflagellates. While some zooflagellates are parasitic, not all of them are parasites. Many zooflagellates are free-living and not parasitic.

Trichonymphas are indeed complex, specialized flagellates, but they are not typically considered zooflagellates. Instead, they are often associated with termites and are part of the flagellate group Trichomonadida.

questions.explanation: A. With single central nucleusB. Enable them to moveD. With many flagella which live as symbionts in guts

questions.text: African sleeping sickness is caused by which of these human parasites?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Choanoflagellates are not known to cause African sleeping sickness. They are a group of protists that are more closely related to the evolutionary ancestors of animals and are typically found in aquatic environments.
This is the correct answer. African sleeping sickness is caused by Trypanosoma, specifically Trypanosoma brucei. These are parasitic flagellate protozoa transmitted by the tsetse fly.

Trichonymphas are specialized flagellate protists that are commonly found in the guts of termites, where they assist in breaking down cellulose. They are not associated with causing African sleeping sickness.
Paramecium is a ciliate protozoan and is not known to cause African sleeping sickness. It is a free-living, freshwater organism.

questions.explanation: A. Sessile marine or fresh water flagellatesC. Complex specialized flagellateD. Ciliate

questions.text: Ciliates are unicellular organisms with a flexible outer covering which is called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The plasmalemma, also known as the cell membrane or plasma membrane, is the semi-permeable outer boundary of a cell that separates the cell's interior from its external environment. It regulates the passage of substances in and out of the cell.

Ciliates, which are a group of protozoa, have a flexible outer covering known as the pellicle. The pellicle is a proteinaceous, semi-rigid layer that provides structural support and flexibility to the cell.
The cuticle is a waxy and protective outer layer found in some organisms, such as the epidermis of plants and the exoskeleton of arthropods, but it is not typically associated with ciliates.

Chitin is a structural polysaccharide found in the exoskeletons of arthropods, the cell walls of fungi, and certain other organisms. It is not the term used to describe the flexible outer covering of ciliates.

questions.explanation: A. Fine,short,hair like structureC. Function in sexual processD. Controls cell metabolism and growth

questions.text: What structures in Foraminiferans and Actinopods, marine protozoans that produce shells, enable the extension of cytoplasmic projections?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Foraminiferans, a type of marine protozoan, often produce shells made of calcium carbonate (calcium). However, calcium itself does not enable the extension of cytoplasmic projections. It's primarily a structural component of their shells.

Actinopods, another group of marine protozoans, are known to produce shells composed of silica. Silica structures, such as radiolarians' intricate skeletons, can provide support for cytoplasmic projections and pseudopodia.
Both Foraminiferans and Actinopods have pores in their shells, which allow for the extension of cytoplasmic projections like pseudopodia. These pores enable them to interact with their environment.

Limestone deposits are primarily associated with the calcium carbonate shells of Foraminiferans. While the shells play a structural role, the actual extension of cytoplasmic projections is facilitated by the pores and other structural features.

questions.explanation: A. Found in shells of foraminiferansB. Found in shells of actinopodsD. Foraminiferans of past have these deposits

questions.text: Plasmodium, the apicomplexan that causes malaria, enters the human bloodstream through the bite of which parasitic protozoan?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Amoebas are not involved in the transmission of malaria. They are a different group of unicellular organisms.
Radiolarians are marine protists and are not associated with the transmission of malaria.
Foraminifera (forams) are also marine protists and have no role in the transmission of malaria.
This is the correct answer. Female Anopheles mosquitoes are known for transmitting Plasmodium parasites, which cause malaria when they bite and feed on human blood.

questions.explanation: A. ZooflagellatesB. ActinopodC. Foraminifera

questions.text: Biologists divide photosynthetic protists based on various aspects. Which one of these pairs, when classified based on pigments, is mismatched?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Brown algae and their pigments include chlorophyll a, chlorophyll c, and carotenes.
Red algae and their pigments include chlorophyll a, carotenes, and phycoerythrin.
Euglenoids and their pigments include chlorophyll a, chlorophyll b, and carotenes.
Green algae and their pigments include chlorophyll a, chlorophyll c, and carotenes.

questions.explanation: A. Brown algaeB. Red algaeC. Euglenoids

questions.text: Which of these statements corresponds to the features of Euglenoids?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Euglenoids have no special evolutionary significance
options.explanation: The evolutionary significance of euglena is the fact that it is considered or has features of both animals and plants. It can photosynthesize using chloroplasts, so it is considered an autotroph. But it also can feed like an animal so it's considered a heterotroph.

Euglena is a genus of unicellular, flagellated microorganisms. They possess characteristics of both plants and animals but are neither placed in the kingdom Plantae nor Animalia. They belong to the kingdom Protista.

Euglena is a genus of unicellular, flagellated microorganisms. They possess characteristics of both plants and animals but are neither placed in the kingdom Plantae nor Animalia. They belong to the kingdom Protista.

options.text: Some photosynthetic euglenoids lose their chlorophyll
options.explanation: Some of the photosynthetic euglenoids lose their chlorophyll when they grow in the dark and obtain nutrients heterotrophically from organic matter. The heterotrophic species of Euglena either absorb organic compounds from the surrounding water or engulf bacteria and protists by phagocytosis within the food vacuoles.

questions.explanation: Some characteristic features of Euglenoids are as follows. • Euglenoids (such as Euglena) are unicellular protists commonly found in freshwater. • Instead of the cell wall, a protein-rich cell membrane known as a pellicle is present. • They bear two flagella on the anterior end of the body. • A small light-sensitive eye spot is present. • They contain photosynthetic pigments such as chlorophyll and can thus prepare their own food. However, in the absence of light, they behave similarly to heterotrophs by capturing other small aquatic organisms. • They have both plant and animal-like features, which makes them difficult to classify and hence they are called connecting links between plants and animals.

questions.text: Dinoflagellates are known to have occasional population blooms or explosions, these blooms frequently color the water red, orange, or brown and are known as:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Kelps are large brown algae and not associated with the red, orange, or brown-colored blooms caused by dinoflagellates.
Polysiphonia is a type of red algae and is not typically responsible for the red tides caused by dinoflagellates.
This is the correct answer. Dinoflagellates are known to cause red tides when they undergo population explosions, leading to the water's discoloration in shades of red, orange, or brown.
Ulva is a type of green algae and is not responsible for the phenomenon of red tides caused by dinoflagellates.

questions.explanation: A. Largest brown algaeB. Red algaeD. Green algae

questions.text: Among protists, which of the following do not have silica deposited in their shells from the options provided?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Diatoms are a type of protist that do have silica deposited in their cell walls, forming intricate and beautiful shells made of silicon dioxide.
Dinoflagellates are a type of protist, and while they have armored plates made of cellulose in their cell walls, they do not have silica deposited in their shells.
Actinopods are marine protists that typically have shells made of silica, making them known for their intricate siliceous skeletons.
Foraminiferans are marine protists that also have shells made of calcium carbonate (calcite), not silica.

questions.explanation: A. Silica deposited in shellB. Cells cover with shells of interlocking cellulose plates impregnated with silicateC. Made up of silica

questions.text: Among the vast variety of protists, which of the following are major producers in aquatic ecosystems?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Amoebas are not typically major producers in aquatic ecosystems. They are heterotrophic protists and feed on other organisms rather than being primary producers.

Euglenophytes, including some Euglenophyta, can contribute to primary production in aquatic ecosystems through photosynthesis. However, their contribution is not as significant as some other protists.
Diatoms are major producers in aquatic ecosystems. They are photosynthetic protists with silica shells and play a significant role in primary production in marine and freshwater environments.
Slime molds are not major producers in aquatic ecosystems. They are more commonly associated with terrestrial environments and have a different ecological role.

questions.explanation: A. Producers second only to diatoms in marine ecosystemB. Often lose their chlorophyllC.Diatoms are responsible for over 40 percent of photosynthesis in the world's oceans, and without them, the ocean would be unable to support the amount of life that it does.D. Alternate source of food

questions.text: Brown algae, like kelps, are the giants of the kingdom Protista. What do they not possess?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Brown algae, including kelps, typically possess blades. Blades are large, leaf-like structures that contain chlorophyll for photosynthesis.
Stripes are not a characteristic of brown algae. They do not possess stripes.
Brown algae, including kelps, have an anchoring structure called a holdfast. The holdfast helps attach them to substrates like rocks.
Rhizopus is not related to brown algae or kelps. It is a genus of filamentous fungi.

questions.explanation: A. Leaf likeB. Stem likeC. Root like

questions.text: Which of the following represents red algae with worldwide distribution?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Laminaria is a type of brown algae, not red algae. It is not representative of red algae with worldwide distribution.
Red tides are not red algae but phenomena caused by various factors, including certain types of dinoflagellates or other protists. They are not representative of red algae.
Polysiphonia is a genus of red algae, and some species have a worldwide distribution. They are representative of red algae with a global presence.
Trypanosoma is a genus of parasitic flagellate protists, and it is not related to red algae.

questions.explanation: A. Brown AlgaeB. DianoflagellatesD. Zooflagellates
hints: represents red algae with worldwide distribution

questions.text: What are the thread-like structures that form the bodies of some protists called?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Holdfast is a structure found in certain algae, particularly brown algae, used for anchoring to substrates. It is not thread-like structures forming the bodies of protists.
The pellicle is a protective outer layer found in some protozoans like paramecia. It is not thread-like structures forming the bodies of protists.
Hyphae are thread-like structures found in fungi, particularly in the fungal body called mycelium. They are not typically associated with protists.

Pseudopodia are temporary, finger-like projections that some protists like amoebas use for movement and feeding. They can be considered thread-like structures and are characteristic of certain protists.

questions.explanation: A. Kelps have this root like structure B. Outer covering of ciliatesD. Found in amoeba

questions.text: All of these groups of protists move with pseudopods except for which one?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Zooflagellates typically move with flagella, not pseudopods. They use whip-like flagella for locomotion.
Amoebas are known for their use of pseudopods (false feet) for movement. They extend and retract pseudopods to propel themselves.
Actinopods, also known as radiolarians, use pseudopods for movement. They have delicate, thread-like pseudopodia for both mobility and capturing prey.
Foraminifera move using pseudopods, extending these temporary projections to change position and capture food particles.

questions.explanation: Psuedopods are temporary projections of the protoplasm, as of certain protozoans, usually serving as an organ of locomotion. Cells that possess this faculty are generally referred to as amoeboids.

questions.text: What is the model organism used to study fundamental biological processes like growth, differentiation, cytoplasmic streaming, and cytoskeleton function?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Phytophthora infestans is a water mold and a plant pathogen. It's not commonly used as a model organism to study fundamental biological processes.

This is the correct answer. Physarum polycephalum, also known as the "slime mold," is often used as a model organism to study various biological processes, including growth, differentiation, cytoplasmic streaming, and cytoskeleton function.
Nostoc is a genus of cyanobacteria, and it is not typically used as a model organism for the mentioned biological processes.
Plasmodium is a genus of parasitic protozoa responsible for malaria. It is not used as a model organism for these processes.

questions.explanation: A. OomycotsC. Subaerial cyanobacterium D. Apicomplexans
hints: biological processes like growth, differentiation, cytoplasmic streaming, and cytoskeleton function

questions.text: Which of the following statements is not true for slime molds in their feeding stage, known as a plasmodium?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: It ingests bacteria, yeasts, spores and decaying organic matter
options.explanation: True. During their feeding stage as a plasmodium, slime molds ingest a variety of microorganisms, including bacteria, yeasts, and organic matter.
True. In response to adverse conditions, slime molds form resistant haploid spores as part of their life cycle.
True. As part of their life cycle, slime molds undergo meiosis to produce haploid spores.

options.text: Their zygote produce single nucleated plasmodium
options.explanation: Not true. The zygote of slime molds typically undergoes mitosis to form a multinucleated plasmodium, which is a characteristic feature of slime mold biology.

questions.explanation: A. As it creeps alongB. Under unfavorable conditionsC. Within stalked structure called sporangia
hints: slime molds in their feeding stage

questions.text: Which of the following protists are considered the ancestors of sponges?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Amoebae are not considered the ancestors of sponges. They are a diverse group of protozoa with no direct evolutionary link to sponges.
Zooflagellates are not typically considered the ancestors of sponges. They are a group of protists with flagella, and their relationship to sponges is not as direct.
Actinopods, also known as radiolarians, are not considered the ancestors of sponges. They are marine protists with intricate silica skeletons.

This is the correct answer. Choanoflagellates are considered the closest living relatives of the choanocyte cells found in sponges. These cells are an essential part of sponge anatomy, suggesting a shared evolutionary lineage between choanoflagellates and sponges.

questions.explanation: Ancestors of today's sponges may have been the first truly multicellular animals. That distinction may belong to protists called Choanoflagellates, which are single or multicellular bouquets or spheres of what looks just like the choanocytes inside.

questions.text: Which protozoa move by flagella?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Amoebae typically move by extending and retracting pseudopods (false feet) rather than using flagella.
Paramecium is a ciliate protozoan that moves using cilia, which are short, hair-like structures, not flagella.
Trypanosomes are flagellate protozoa. They have one or more whip-like flagella that they use for locomotion.
Plasmodium is a non-motile parasitic protozoan and does not move by flagella. It is responsible for causing malaria in humans.

questions.explanation: Trypanosomes move actively and progress by the movement of the undulating membrane and the free flagellum, which acts as a kind of propeller, thus drawing themselves through the blood plasma or tissue fluid.

questions.text: What causes amoebic dysentery in humans?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Amoeba proteus is not the cause of amoebic dysentery in humans. It is a harmless amoeba commonly used as a model organism in biology.
Naegleria fowleri is a pathogenic amoeba, but it causes a rare and severe brain infection known as primary amoebic meningoencephalitis (PAM), not amoebic dysentery.
Trichonympha is a type of flagellate protozoan that lives in the intestines of termites. It is not associated with amoebic dysentery in humans.

This is the correct answer. Entamoeba histolytica is the protozoan responsible for causing amoebic dysentery in humans. It is a parasitic amoeba that can lead to intestinal infections and dysentery when ingested.

questions.explanation: Amoebic dysentery is an infection caused by any of the amoebae of the Entamoeba group. Symptoms are most common during infection by Entamoeba histolytica. Ciliates may reproduce sexually (conjugation) or asexually (fission)

questions.text: Sexual process of ciliates is controlled by?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Micronuclei play a crucial role in the sexual reproduction of ciliates. They are involved in processes like conjugation and the exchange of genetic material between ciliates.
Macronuclei are essential for the day-to-day metabolic and physiological functions of ciliates. However, they are not directly involved in the sexual reproduction process.

Sex pili, or conjugation pili, are structures used by ciliates during conjugation for cell-to-cell contact. They are essential for the exchange of genetic material between ciliates but do not control the sexual process on their own.
According to PTB, page no 103 under the heading of Ciliates, small diploid micronuclei function in metabolism ans growth. While the macronucleus controls cell metabolism and growth.

questions.explanation: During conjugation two ciliates come into contact with each other forming a cytoplasmic bridge between them. This is followed by a process called meiosis of the micronuclei of either cell to producer haploid micronuclei.

questions.text: The shell of foraminifera is made up of?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The shell of foraminifera is primarily composed of calcium carbonate (calcite). This mineral forms the protective and often intricately shaped shells of these marine protists.
Calcium hydroxide is not a significant component of foraminifera shells. These shells are primarily made of calcium carbonate.
Silica is a component of the shells of other marine protists, such as diatoms and some radiolarians. Foraminifera shells are primarily composed of calcium carbonate.
Chitin is a structural component found in the exoskeletons of arthropods and the cell walls of fungi. It is not present in the shells of foraminifera.

questions.explanation: Forams are unusual among single-celled organisms because they build shells made up of calcium carbonate (calcareous) or from tiny grains stuck together.

questions.text: Ceratium is an example of?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ceratium is not an example of Euglena. Euglena is a different type of single-celled protist with a distinctive shape and a single whip-like flagellum.
Ceratium is not a diatom. Diatoms are a group of photosynthetic protists with intricate silica shells, whereas Ceratium is a type of dinoflagellate.
Ceratium is not an example of brown algae. Brown algae are multicellular seaweeds, while Ceratium is a type of unicellular dinoflagellate.
This is the correct answer. Ceratium is a type of dinoflagellate, which is a group of unicellular protists known for their diverse and often complex shapes, including Ceratium.

questions.explanation: Ceratium, genus of single-celled dinoflagellate algae common in fresh water and saltwater from the Arctic to the tropics.

questions.text: Which of the following are the most numerous unicellular algae in the ocean?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Spirogyra is a filamentous green alga, but it is not the most numerous unicellular algae in the ocean. It is commonly found in freshwater environments.
Volvox is a type of colonial green alga. It forms spherical colonies and is not typically found in the ocean.
Diatoms are the correct answer. They are the most numerous unicellular algae in the ocean. They are a major component of phytoplankton and play a vital role in marine ecosystems.
Kelps are not unicellular algae. They are large, brown seaweeds, typically found in marine environments, but they are multicellular and not among the most numerous unicellular algae in the ocean.

questions.explanation: Diatoms are the photosynthetic unicellular algae populating the oceans.

questions.text: Which of the following forms a common monophyletic lineage with plants?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Green algae, particularly the Chlorophyta group, forms a common monophyletic lineage with plants. They share many characteristics, including chlorophylls and other pigments, and are considered closely related to land plants.
Brown algae, such as kelps, belong to a different lineage and are not closely related to plants. They have differences in their photosynthetic pigments and life history.
Red algae also belong to a different lineage from green plants, and they are not considered closely related to land plants.
This is not the correct answer. While green algae share a monophyletic lineage with land plants, brown and red algae belong to different evolutionary lineages and are not closely related to plants.

questions.explanation: The green algae and land plants form a monophyletic lineage (the chlorophytes) that contain both Protista and higher taxa. Green algae contain the same carotenoids and chlorophyll a and b as land plants, whereas other land plants have different accessory pigments and types of chlorophylls molecules in addition to chlorophyll a. Both green plants and land plants also store carbohydrates as starch. Pyrenoids are sub-cellular micro-compartments found in chloroplasts of many algae.

questions.text: What acts as a site for starch formation and storage in green algae?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Vacuoles in green algae serve various functions, including storage of water and waste products, but they are not the primary site for starch formation and storage.

Chloroplasts are the organelles primarily responsible for photosynthesis in green algae and other photosynthetic organisms. While they are involved in starch synthesis, they are not the primary storage site for starch.
The cytoplasm is the general cellular fluid where various cellular processes occur, but it is not the primary site for starch formation and storage.

This is the correct answer. Pyrenoids are specialized structures found within the chloroplasts of many green algae. They function as the primary site for starch formation and storage. Pyrenoids play a crucial role in the synthesis and storage of starch in these organisms.

questions.explanation: It is the site of starch formation and storage.

questions.text: What type of algae is harvested as fertilizer worldwide?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Red algae are not commonly harvested as fertilizer. They are often used in the production of agar and carrageenan, which are food additives and thickening agents.
Green algae are not typically harvested for use as fertilizer. They are used more frequently in research and various industries.

This is the correct answer. Brown algae, particularly types like kelp, are commonly harvested and used as fertilizer in many parts of the world. They are rich in nutrients and can improve soil quality.
Diatoms, while rich in silica, are not commonly harvested as fertilizer. They are more commonly used in industries such as filtration and abrasives.

questions.explanation: Brown algae include a number of edible seaweeds. Seaweeds use as agricultural fertilizers the extracts from brown algae have increased the yield of crops, seed germination, resistance to frost, fungal and insect attacks and, uptake of inorganic constituents.

questions.text: Which of the following pigment(s) is/are present in Euglenoids?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Chlorophyll a is present in Euglenoids, and it is the primary photosynthetic pigment used for capturing light energy during photosynthesis.
Euglenoids do not typically contain chlorophyll b. Chlorophyll b is more commonly found in green algae and land plants.
Carotenoids are present in Euglenoids and play a role in photosynthesis. They assist in capturing a wider range of light wavelengths.
This is not the correct answer. Euglenoids do not typically contain chlorophyll

questions.explanation: The chlorophylls of Euglenoids contain chlorophyll a and chlorophyll b. Euglenoids also have some carotenoid pigments.

questions.text: Flagella is absent in?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Diatoms typically have flagella, though they are often very small and not easily visible. Some diatoms are motile and possess flagella.
Brown algae do not have flagella. They rely on other means, such as water currents, for movement.
Green algae can have flagella, but they are not present in all species. The presence or absence of flagella varies among different green algae.
Red algae do not have flagella. They lack flagellated stages in their life cycle and have other mechanisms for reproduction and dispersal.

questions.explanation: Red algae do not have flagella or centrioles during their entire life cycle.

questions.text: What is a unicellular motile alga?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Chlorella is a unicellular green alga, but it is not typically motile. It is often used in research and as a dietary supplement.
Desmids are unicellular green algae that have distinctive and often symmetrical shapes, but they are not motile.

This is the correct answer. Chlamydomonas is a unicellular, motile green alga. It has two flagella, which allow it to move through the water and is commonly used as a model organism in biological research.
Spirogyra is a filamentous green alga and is not unicellular. It forms long, spiral-shaped filaments and is not typically motile.

questions.explanation: Desmids, chlorella and spirogyra lack flagella.Chlamydomonas are unicellular algae and have flagella.

questions.text: To which group does Pinnularia belong?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Pinnularia belongs to the diatom group within the Chrysophyta (or Heterokontophyta) division. Diatoms are a type of golden-brown algae.
Pyrophyta is an older term for fire algae, which is not associated with Pinnularia.
Pinnularia is not a member of the Rodophyta (red algae) group.
Pinnularia is not a member of the Phaeophyta (brown algae) group.

questions.explanation: Pinnolaria is a genus fresh water alga more specifically a type of diatom.

questions.text: The cell wall of Oomycotes is chemically made up of?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The cell wall of Oomycotes is chemically made up of cellulose, not chitin. Chitin is typically found in the cell walls of fungi.
Oomycotes do not have cell walls made up of proteins. Instead, their cell walls consist of cellulose and other polysaccharides.
Lignin is not a major component of Oomycote cell walls. It is a complex polymer found in the cell walls of vascular plants.

This is the correct answer. The cell wall of Oomycotes is primarily composed of cellulose, a polysaccharide made up of glucose units. Cellulose is a key structural component of the Oomycote cell wall.

questions.explanation: The cell wall of oomycetes, however, is not composed of chitin, as in the fungi, but is made up of a mix of cellulosic compounds and glycan.

questions.text: Hyphae of Oomycotes are?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Oomycotes typically do not have septate hyphae. They have aseptate (non-septate) hyphae.

questions.text: The function of the contractile vacuole in protozoa is:

questions.explanation: Amoeba forms a cyst and reproduces by multiple fission during adverse environmental conditions. The animal secretes a three layered, protective, chitinous cyst around it and becomes inactive. Inside the cyst, the nucleus repeatedly divides to form several daughter nuclei, which arrange themselves near the periphery. Each daughter nucleus becomes enveloped by a small amount of cytoplasm, thus forming a daughter amoeba, called amoebula or pseudopodiospore. When favourable conditions arrive, the cyst breaks off liberating the young pseudopodiospores, each with fine pseudopodia. They feed and grow rapidly to become adults and lead an independent life.
